title: Description of pull option in command line help for run command doesn't contain allowed values
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

When using the `podman run` command, I wanted to know how to set the pull policy, so I tried to look it up using `podman run --help`. However the output for this option doesn't contain all the possible values (see below), contrary to the output of other options such as sdnotify. So I had to look it up in the documentation, where it was thankfully described.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run the following command:
$ podman run --help
2. Look for the description of the pull option

**Describe the results you received:**
Among other option descriptions, the following was printed for the pull option:
>      --pull string                              Pull image policy (default "missing")
 The description in the documentation of podman lists all possible values, but as you can see, the command line help does not.

**Describe the results you expected:**
When using docker as a comparison (sorry), the following is printed there:
>      --pull string                    Pull image before running ("always"|"missing"|"never") (default "missing")
I expected something similar for podman. Other options that can have multiple possible values list them all. Suggestion for the description of the pull option:
>      --pull string                              Pull image policy ("always"|"missing"|"never"|"newer") (default "missing")

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
-

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Built:        Fri Nov 11 16:01:27 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

I don't think that's relevant here.

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.3.1-1.fc37.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Fedora 37 default installation

comments.body: Thanks for reaching out, @TheHaf.  I opened https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16847 to add the supported policies to the help messages.
